data.title: GitHub unveils BYOK feature for heightened security, giving admins more control over access. #Enterprise #GitHub #BYOK
data.description: GitHub has upgraded enterprise security by introducing Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) for GitHub Models. This enhancement provides administrators with increased authority over organizational access, improving overall security measures. This step signifies a significant advancement in ensuring secure access to vital company data.

data.title: Alabama CBD customers can visit open Sunmed™ | Your CBD Store® locations, carrying full inventory of premium hemp products.
data.description: Sunmed™ | Your CBD Store® is pleased to announce the opening of its locations in Alabama as of August 1, 2025. These outlets offer a wide selection of premium hemp and CBD products that are compliant, clinically proven, and of high quality. Customers in Alabama can now access a full inventory of these products at their convenience.

data.title: Shiba Inu's 5th birthday shows burn rate spiking to 5,800%, signaling potential breakout; SHIB price drops by 10.5% in a week.
data.description: Shiba Inu (SHIB) celebrated its fifth anniversary amidst a surge in bullish sentiment, with indications from on-chain activity and burn data suggesting a potential breakout. Currently, the cryptocurrency is priced at $0.00001227, showing a 10.5% decrease over the past seven days. In comparison, Dogecoin (DOGE) stands at $0.2077, marking a 10.2% drop, while Pepe (PEPE) is at $0.00001058, reflecting a 14% decline. Noteworthy traders in the crypto space have shared insights on the market. Vegeta highlights the robust monthly chart structure of Shiba Inu, emphasizing sustained demand over months, hinting at an imminent major movement. On the other hand, Trader Joe Swanson observes the formation of a classic cup-and-handle pattern. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cryptocurrency markets down as Bitcoin hits $115,000, Ethereum at $3,646. XRP and Dogecoin attempt bounce. Fed rate cut hopes spark rebound.
data.description: Cryptocurrency markets experienced a dip on Friday morning before bouncing back due to optimism surrounding a potential September rate cut by the Federal Reserve following a weak jobs report. The prices for major cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $115,487, Ethereum at $3,646, Solana at $168.37, XRP at $2.99, Dogecoin at $0.207, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001227. Notable statistics include an 8.6% increase in large transactions for Bitcoin and a 6.5% decrease for Ethereum. Daily active addresses rose by 5.3% for Bitcoin and dropped by 0.1% for Ethereum. Data from Coinglass revealed that 184,797 traders were liquidated, amounting to $758.65 million in the past 24 hours. SoSoValue data indicated net outflows of $114.8 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Wednesday, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw net inflows of $17 million. Observations from Stockmoney Lizards suggested that Bitcoin has partially closed its fair value gap and initiated a trading pattern. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: IHS Towers to release Q2 2025 earnings on August 12 at 6am ET, with conference call scheduled at 8.30am ET on same day.
data.description: IHS Holding Limited (NYSE: IHS) has announced that it will release its financial results for the three-month period ending June 30, 2025 (2Q25 Earnings Results) at approximately 6am ET (11am UK time) on Tuesday, August 12, 2025. A conference call and webcast to review the 2Q25 Earnings Results are scheduled for 8.30am ET (1.30pm UK time) on the same day.

data.title: Toll Brothers to launch luxury home community in Scottsdale, Arizona with single-level home designs. Sales start fall 2025.
data.description: Luxury home builder Toll Brothers, Inc. has revealed plans for a new luxury home community in Scottsdale, Arizona. The upcoming Toll Brothers at Storyrock development, situated in the esteemed Storyrock master plan, will feature single-level home designs on spacious home sites boasting picturesque mountain views. Sales for this exclusive gated neighborhood are set to kick off in the fall of 2025. Toll Brothers, Inc., known as the top builder of luxury homes nationwide and listed on the NYSE as TOL, anticipates a strong reception for this new project.

data.title: Slow hiring due to Trump tariffs may lead to Fed rate cut; S&P 500 falls. Unemployment rise. Fed cut likely.
data.description: A decrease in hiring rates and an increase in unemployment figures may increase the possibility of a rate reduction. The impact of Trump's tariffs has caused a slowdown in hiring, leading to a potential cut in interest rates by the Federal Reserve. This has also resulted in a decline in the S&P 500 index. The situation is being closely monitored by investors and financial analysts.

data.title: Lido DAO's price falls 6.59% to $0.94 amid market share worries despite institutional interest and technical support. ETH staking now at 25%.
data.description: The price of LDO is currently at $0.94, which represents a decrease of 6.59%. The Ethereum staking market share has dropped to 25%, leading to a bearish market trend. This is occurring despite significant recent interest from institutional investors and the presence of strong technical support.

data.title: BCE acquires Ziply Fiber to accelerate fibre growth strategy, expanding Bell's footprint. Read Caution Concerning Forward-Looking Statements for related risks and assumptions.
data.description: The acquisition discussed in this news release involves forward-looking statements. For a comprehensive understanding of associated risk factors and assumptions, refer to the "Caution Concerning Forward-Looking Statements" section later in this release. The acquisition significantly broadens Bell's fiber footprint in the United States, reflecting a strategic move for the company. Key numbers including the monetary value of the acquisition and the extent of the expanded fiber network were not explicitly mentioned.

data.title: HCI to host SPARK TALENT 2025 conference in San Antonio on Oct 7-9, focusing on workforce planning, talent acquisition & AI in HR.
data.description: In Brentwood, Tennessee, on August 1, 2025, the Human Capital Institute (HCI) revealed details about SPARK TALENT 2025, a top-tier event focusing on recruiting and workforce strategies. The conference is scheduled for October 7-9 at the Grand Hyatt San Antonio River Walk in San Antonio, Texas. SPARK TALENT aims to bring together professionals to discuss the latest trends and best practices in talent acquisition and management.
